rationale: Iran's Hormuz reopening signal and congressional pushback on Iran deal remain active fronts directly involving US foreign policy; China arresting a US academic adds fresh friction; SpaceX market debut is a notable financial event.

top_events.title: Deal to end fighting would lead to Hormuz reopening, Iran says / UAE unlocks frozen Iranian funds
top_events.summary: Iran signals Hormuz reopening tied to a ceasefire deal while UAE unlocks frozen funds — active US diplomatic engagement with congressional pushback creates real policy tension affecting oil markets and US foreign commitments.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for espionage activities
top_events.summary: China detaining a US academic for alleged espionage adds a fresh friction point to US-China tensions, raising concerns for Americans traveling or working in China and signaling continued bilateral deterioration.

top_events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
top_events.summary: SpaceX's stock market debut and Musk's trillionaire milestone are significant US financial market events, with broad implications for investor sentiment and tech sector valuations.
